summary: Tested backend APIs (pytest, 8/8 passing) and full frontend landing page for Devil on the Hudson. All sections (Hero, Covenant, Trident, Author, Reviews, Final Reconquest) render with required data-testid attributes. Subscription flow works end-to-end: valid email shows success toast, duplicate email shows 'already inscribed' toast. Invalid email is blocked by browser-native HTML5 email validation (input type=email) before reaching the app's custom toast logic — minor UX gap.

integration_issues.issue: Custom error toast ('Inscribe a true email upon the parchment.') is unreachable because <input type='email'> triggers browser-native HTML5 validation tooltip first and blocks form submission. The regex check in handleSubmit never executes for typical invalid inputs. Consider adding noValidate to the form or switching type to 'text' so the custom toast renders.

action_items: Optional: Add noValidate to <form data-testid='letter-of-marque-form'> (or switch email input type to 'text') so the in-app custom invalid-email toast is reachable. Functionality currently works via browser validation, so this is low priority.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: emails are normalized via .lower().strip() on insert/lookup — good duplicate handling and case-insensitive matching verified.
server.py: subscribe endpoint correctly excludes _id from response (no Mongo ObjectId leak).
Frontend uses process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL correctly with /api prefix.
All interactive elements carry data-testid attributes as specified.
FinalReconquest.jsx: the regex validation after .trim() is shadowed by the browser's built-in HTML5 email validation; consider noValidate or rely solely on backend 422 handling.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all required testids present, all 3 subscribe flows functionally validated; invalid case handled by browser instead of toast)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ prefixed subscriber documents during pytest runs and runtime sailor_* emails during Playwright tests in MongoDB subscribers collection. Safe to leave; or main agent may clean via prefix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_landing_api.py covers: root welcome, valid subscribe, duplicate subscribe, case-insensitive duplicate, invalid email (422), missing email (422), subscribe with custom source, count endpoint. Frontend covered Hero CTAs/Amazon link/Explore-the-Legend scroll/Covenant/Trident pillars/Author/Reviews (Elma Bruin verified)/FinalReconquest form/footer wax seals/audio-toggle. Only minor finding: HTML5 native validation intercepts invalid emails before the custom toast.
rca of the issue: Invalid-email custom toast not displayed → Root cause: <input type='email'> in FinalReconquest.jsx triggers browser-native validity check on submit, which blocks form submission and shows a native tooltip. The handleSubmit's regex branch and toast.error call only run for values that pass HTML5 validation but fail the stricter regex (effectively never). Mitigation: add `noValidate` to the form OR change input type to 'text' (keeping aria-label='Email') to allow the in-app toast UX to surface.
